title: Application Metadata, Transactions, Events, and Orphans · Execution Trace: Orphan Repair
summary: A database row says ready before S3 accepts bytes, an object exists without an owner row after rollback, and events race ahead of scan and authorization state. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the orphan repair increment in file metadata and object reconciliation state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Detect and safely repair orphan objects, orphan rows, stale upload intents, missing scans, and mismatched versions under bounded listing cost.
Publish file events from committed state with outbox, stable identity, duplicate-safe consumers, and observed object-version evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Orphan Repair · Theory Orphan Repair
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 2823 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Orphan Repair contract for the supplied data.
